description_long: This object is a small, round, reddish-brown clay oil lamp with a wide, shallow bowl and a slightly raised base. The lamp features a single, prominent white flame emerging from a blackened wick holder at one edge of the bowl. The bowl's interior is smooth and open, designed to hold oil, while the exterior has a matte, unglazed finish typical of traditional terracotta. The flame is upright and emits a faint glow, suggesting the lamp is lit and in use. The overall design is simple and functional, with a rustic aesthetic suitable for both decorative and practical purposes in urban outdoor or indoor settings.
description: A small, round terracotta oil lamp with a wide bowl and a single white flame emerging from a black wick holder. The lamp has a rustic, matte finish and is designed for tabletop use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full round bowl and the upright white flame at the center edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the protruding black wick holder and the flame extending from the bowl's rim.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open bowl and the flame directly above the center, with the wick holder less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the black wick holder and flame on the right edge of the bowl.
